description_long: This object consists of three traditional wooden cabins arranged side by side, each featuring steeply pitched thatched gable roofs with visible wooden beams and crossbars. The cabins are constructed from vertical wooden planks, with stone foundations providing a sturdy base. The roofs are thick and textured, suggesting insulation and weather resistance, and the overhanging eaves add to the rustic appearance. Each cabin has a symmetrical facade with a central door and multiple small, rectangular windows, framed by dark wooden trim. The overall design evokes historical rural architecture, likely inspired by Northern European or Japanese minka styles. The cabins are compact, with no visible porches or external staircases, and are set on a rough, stone-like ground surface.
description: A group of three rustic wooden cabins with steep thatched gable roofs, stone foundations, and symmetrical facades featuring central doors and small windows.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the three cabins' thatched roofs and their linear arrangement, with roof beams and eaves clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep pitch of the roofs, the wooden plank walls, and the stone foundations of all three cabins.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the central cabin, with the thatched roof and wooden wall details prominent.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of doors and windows on each cabin, as well as the stone bases.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable thatched roof with exposed wooden beams and overhanging eaves
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small rectangular windows symmetrically arranged on the front and side facades of each cabin
attribute_door_placement: single central door on the front facade of each cabin
